description:
Advances: specled.binding

Deliverable:
Add a path-aware sibling to `SpecLedEx.Realization.Binding.resolve/2` that returns the resolved AST (or `{:module, mod}` for bare-module bindings) alongside the source file path that defines the binding. Promote the existing private `locate_source/2` to public. The path is derived from `Module.module_info(:compile)[:source]` (beam-path) or the source-fallback path, normalized so it can be compared against repository-relative file paths via `Path.relative_to/2`. The path may be `nil` when neither lookup yields one (e.g. hot-reloaded modules); callers treat `nil` as 'no attestation possible.'

The existing `resolve/2` signature does NOT change — its three callers (api_boundary check_binding, implementation resolve_owned_mfas / resolve_shared_mfas, orchestrator compute_seed_hashes) stay untouched.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: tagged_tests for specled.binding.resolve_with_source_returns_path, specled.binding.resolve_with_source_handles_use_generated, specled.binding.locate_source_public in test/specled_ex/realization/binding_test.exs. Flip those entries' execute: false to execute: true in .spec/specs/binding.spec.md.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: scenarios specled.binding.scenario.resolve_with_source_beam_path, specled.binding.scenario.resolve_with_source_use_generated, specled.binding.scenario.resolve_with_source_path_unknown, specled.binding.scenario.locate_source_public_callable.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex
- lib/specled_ex/realization/orchestrator.ex
- lib/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary.ex
- lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex
- .spec/specs/branch_guard.spec.md
- .spec/specs/realized_by.spec.md

Allowed touches (this ticket may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/realization/binding.ex
- test/specled_ex/realization/binding_test.exs
- .spec/specs/binding.spec.md (only to flip execute: false → execute: true on the three new requirements)

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT change `resolve/2`'s public signature. Adding a new function and exposing locate_source/2 are the only API changes.
- Do NOT propagate the source path into existing callers (api_boundary, implementation, orchestrator drift checks). Those callers continue to use `resolve/2` as today; the new function is for attestation only.
- Do NOT introduce any caching layer. `Code.ensure_loaded/1` is already paid for elsewhere; premature optimization is out of scope.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification entries flipped to execute: true
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Session: b920bd00-78bf-43a9-b891-877e668cc527

- lib/specled_ex/realization/binding.ex (Allowed)
- test/specled_ex/realization/binding_test.exs (Allowed)
- .spec/specs/binding.spec.md (Allowed — execute: false → execute: true flip)
- .spec/state.json (derived; rewritten by mix spec.check)

Tests added (all carrying @tag spec for the requirement under test):
- "resolve_with_source/2 returns {:ok, ast, path} where path is the beam-derived source file"
- "resolve_with_source/2 returns {:ok, {:module, mod}, path} for bare-module bindings"
- "resolve_with_source/2 normalizes a project-root path against the current working directory"
- "resolve_with_source/2 returns {:error, :not_found, _} when the MFA cannot be located"
- "resolve_with_source/2 returns a nil source path for a module compiled without a real source file"
- "resolve_with_source/2 — use-generated functions returns the path of the module that invoked `use`, not the DSL module's file"
- "locate_source/2 — public is publicly callable and returns {:ok, path} for a loaded module"
- "locate_source/2 — public accepts a nil context and a %Context{} interchangeably"
- "locate_source/2 — public returns :error when neither the manifest nor module_info yields a real source"

Verification stubs flipped: the tagged_tests entry covering specled.binding.resolve_with_source_returns_path / .resolve_with_source_handles_use_generated / .locate_source_public is now execute: true.

Summary: Added path-aware resolver sibling `resolve_with_source/2` that returns AST plus a project-root-normalized source path (via Path.relative_to/2), and promoted `locate_source/2` from private to public. The path source is `module_info(:compile)[:source]` (beam path) with `locate_source/2` source-fallback; the sentinel "nofile" emitted by dynamic-compilation paths (`Code.compile_string`, `Code.compile_quoted`) is treated as "no real source" and produces a nil path, matching the spec's "hot-reloaded modules without :source" condition. The existing `resolve/2` signature is unchanged — no callers in api_boundary / implementation / orchestrator were touched.

Local sanity:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean
- mix test test/specled_ex/realization/binding_test.exs: 17 tests, 0 failures
- mix test (full suite): 782 tests, 0 failures
- mix format --check-formatted on touched files: clean
- mix deps.unlock --check-unused: clean
- mix spec.check: the 3 binding requirement errors from this ticket cleared (errors 10 → 7; remaining 7 are branch_guard + realized_by from specled_-3rs.{2,3,4}, out of scope here).

Project-wide `mix format --check-formatted` reports `lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex` is unformatted — this is pre-existing on the epic branch (verified by stashing my changes) and is in Out of Scope for this ticket.

- Requirements with passing tests: 11/11 musts on specled.binding (the 3 new + 8 pre-existing)
  - resolve_with_source_returns_path: 5 tests with @tag spec (beam-derived path, bare-module, project-relative normalization via Path.relative_to, not-found, nil-for-nofile)
  - resolve_with_source_handles_use_generated: 1 test with @describetag spec — fixture compiles `SomeDSL` + `PhoenixLikeConsumer` (which `use`s the DSL) into one source file and asserts `handle_event/2` resolves to the consumer's file path
  - locate_source_public: 3 tests with @tag/@describetag spec — public callable, nil-vs-Context interchangeable, :error on nofile-sentinel module
- Intent-level scope concerns: none
  - mix.exs / mix.lock unchanged (no new external deps)
  - No caching layer introduced (only the already-paid `Code.ensure_loaded/1`)
  - resolve/2 callers untouched
- Project verification:
  - mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean
  - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/binding_test.exs: 17 tests, 0 failures
  - mix test (full suite): 2 properties, 782 tests, 0 failures
  - mix format --check-formatted on touched files (binding.ex, binding_test.exs): clean
  - mix deps.unlock --check-unused: clean
  - mix spec.check: 7 errors / 7 warnings, ALL on `specled.branch_guard` and `specled.realized_by` (specled_-3rs.{2,3,4} territory, in this ticket's Out of Scope). Zero findings against `specled.binding`. Matches implementer's "errors 10 → 7" claim.

Source-claim provenance (highest-risk verification): the spec asserts the source path is derived from `Module.module_info(:compile)[:source]` and normalized via `Path.relative_to/2`. Verified both definition AND consumption:
- `mod.module_info(:compile)[:source]` is invoked at lib/specled_ex/realization/binding.ex:188 (`beam_compile_source/1`, called by `source_path_for/2` from `resolve_with_source/2`) and at line 380 (`infer_source_path/1`, called by `locate_source/2`).
- `Path.relative_to(path, cwd)` is invoked at line 218 (`normalize_path/1`), called from both `source_path_for/2` and both `locate_source/2` clauses.
- The "nofile" sentinel (from `Code.compile_string/2` etc.) is treated as :error by `nonfile_or_ok/1` (line 205), so the resolver returns nil for hot-reloaded modules — matching the spec's "path may be nil when neither lookup yields one."
- The test "returns a nil source path for a module compiled without a real source file" exercises this end-to-end with a real `Code.compile_string`-produced module whose `module_info(:compile)[:source]` is asserted to be "nofile" before calling the resolver.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- The pre-existing project-wide `mix format` issue on `lib/specled_ex/review/html.ex` (and ~15 other files) is unrelated to this ticket and exists on the base commit; touched files are themselves formatted.
- The `.spec/state.json` change is the tooling-regenerated index/findings reflecting the 3 cleared requirement_without_test_tag errors. No human-authored content in state.json was touched.
- All Merge gate items from the ticket are satisfied except the auditor APPROVE comment (this comment serves that role).

title: Path-aware binding resolver
